references.title: Predicting Students’ Algebra I Performance using Reinforcement Learning with Multi-Group Fairness

extracted.Data sources: LMS logs, Exam Grade, Learner Demographics
extracted.Features: [StudentNumber, FRL_Status, Hispanic_Indicator, Rade_Indicator, SchoolNumber, Video watching, learning resource reading, leaderboard checking, etc…, number of homework questions, number of correct questions]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Authors do a different approach as they intend to explore the idea of fairness. 

Analysis of results takes into account multi-group fairness - how distributions of certain attributes may affect the “predictions” and performance of a model. 

This fairness evoluations is conducted by the selection of an approppriate metric,
extracted.Models: kNN, SVM, Gaussian Naive Bayes, Logistic Regression, MLP
extracted.Comments:
Authors do a standard ML pipeline where not only do they solve a regular classification problem but afterward try to do an analysis of Bias using BPSN. 

This analysis is subsequently confirmed with a Reinforcement Learning approach.
extracted.Limitations:
Specific to an Algebra Context.

NO XAI analysis.

annotations.text: Algorithmic bias in LA is an underexplored topic. Study proposes an algorithmic strategy meant to reduce algorithmic bias in a multi-group context. Results show promise
